name: Renaissance Pizza
recipeIngredient: 2 cups carrots; peeled and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
Sauce: In a large skillet, sauté carrots, onions, garlic, and celery in olive oil for 5 minutes. Add seasonings and sauté another 5 minutes. Stir in sweetener, then add tomato puree and simmer for 1 to 2 hours until thickened (about 7 cups total).
Dough: Dissolve yeast in warm water with sweetener; let stand until foamy. In a large bowl, combine flour and yeast mixture. Add olive oil, salt, and remaining water; mix into a medium stiff dough. Divide into two pieces, lightly oil two large rectangular pans, sprinkle with cornmeal, and roll each piece to an even rectangle. Let rise about 1 hour or until doubled in size. Punch down the dough before baking.
Spice Mix: In a bowl, combine basil, oregano, parsley, garlic powder, black pepper.
Vegetable Topping: Sauté mixed vegetables (onions, mushrooms, broccoli stems and florets, cauliflower pieces) in oil for 4 to 5 minutes. Remove from heat and transfer to another pan if not using immediately.
Assembly: Spread about 1 1/2 cups sauce on each crust. Add about 3 cups vegetable topping and sprinkle with spice mixture. Optional: spread shredded soy cheese over the top. Bake at 375°F for about 10 minutes, or longer until cooked through (about 20 minutes).
